fr.label: FR
fr.heroTitle: Deux flux nets. Une archive compacte.
fr.heroCopy: Briefings quotidiens sur l'IA, la robotique et la géopolitique technologique, avec bascule de langue et lecture d'archives.

eng.label: EN
eng.heroTitle: Two sharp feeds. One compact archive.
eng.heroCopy: Daily briefings on AI, robotics, and tech geopolitics, with language switching and archive browsing.

subtitle.fr: Intelligence artificielle, robots et hype technologique
subtitle.eng: Artificial intelligence, robotics, and tech hype

classificationRules: Use AIz for stories mainly about AI, robotics, models, labs, benchmarks, launches, deployments, compute, GPUs, chips, software, cloud capacity, connected devices, product capability, or the business and technical stack around them.

If a government, regulator, military, parliament, sanctions regime, export-control fight, industrial-policy dispute, or geopolitical actor appears but the story concerns AI, GPUs, chips, software, cloud, computers, robotics, connected devices, model access, or the technology stack, keep it in AIz.

subtitle.fr: Geopolitique, conflits, diplomatie et tech comme force stratégique
subtitle.eng: Geopolitics, conflicts, diplomacy, and tech as strategic force

classificationRules: Use Geopolitikz only when the primary frame is geopolitics without a core AI, GPU, computing, software, cloud, chip, connected-device, or robotics angle: diplomacy, war, ceasefires, territorial disputes, sanctions unrelated to technology, state coercion, international regulation, national-security confrontation, or interstate rivalry.

If a story can be read primarily as an AI, GPU, chip, software, computing, cloud, connected-device, robotics, model, or technology-infrastructure story, it belongs in AIz rather than Geopolitikz, even when politicians, militaries, regulators, sanctions, export controls, or China/U.S. rivalry are involved.
